steps.title: Recognize that vertex form displays the minimum
steps.content: The function $f(x) = x^2 - 4x - 320$ is a parabola opening upward (positive leading coefficient). For an upward-opening parabola, the vertex form $f(x) = a(x-h)^2 + k$ displays the minimum value directly as the constant $k$. The question asks which form shows this minimum value explicitly as a constant or coefficient.

steps.title: Complete the square to find vertex form
steps.content:
Starting with $f(x) = x^2 - 4x - 320$:
Take half the coefficient of $x$: $-4 \div 2 = -2$, then square it: $(-2)^2 = 4$.
Add and subtract this value inside:
$$f(x) = (x^2 - 4x + 4) - 4 - 320 = (x-2)^2 - 324$$
The minimum value is now displayed as $-324$.

steps.title: Identify the matching choice
steps.content: The vertex form $(x-2)^2 - 324$ can be written as $(x-2)^2 + (-324)$, which matches choice B. To verify it is equivalent, expand: $(x-2)^2 + (-324) = x^2 - 4x + 4 - 324 = x^2 - 4x - 320$ ✓. This is the only form displaying the minimum value $-324$ as a constant. That matches choice B — B is correct.
